behavior_summary: Provides the 'U8P_LAMPPOST::look' (examine) handler for the lamppost object. When the player examines the lamppost, the process emits a single short bark text "lamp " and then yields/suspends.
No branching, menu interaction, flag reads/writes, or external calls are present. The routine is a minimal, single-purpose description/emote responder.
notable_control_flow: Single straight-line flow: display bark text "lamp " then suspend/yield the process.
No conditional logic, loops, or callouts — deterministic on every examine invocation.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "lamp " intentional formatting or an artifact of extraction?
Does any engine-level behavior occur after the suspend (resume semantics) that affects this object? The script itself yields but performs no explicit cleanup or state change.
Is this U8P_LAMPPOST::look handler ever invoked by fast-area entry semantics in addition to direct player examine actions? (Classification suggests 'U8P_LAMPPOST::look' only.)

main_function.name: U8P_LAMPPOST::look
main_function.purpose: Show the lamppost's short examine description to the player by emitting a single bark text and yielding.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player uses the U8P_LAMPPOST::look/examine action on the lamppost (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_LAMPPOST::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_LAMPPOST::look handler that displays a short description (bark) for the lamppost.
